Ad Text:
Senior Functional Consultant - Oracle EPM (Health Manager Level 4)

Permanent Full-Time
Work from our modern offices in Chatswood, St Leonards, or Charlestown Office
In-office presence with hybrid flexibility for work-life balance
Attractive salary, from $147,653 up to $175,917 per annum plus 12% super and 17.5% annual leave loading
Up to 12 extra days off per year + 4 weeks annual leave
Increase your take home pay with salary packaging, discounted fitness passport and more!
Join the team enriching health in millions of ways every day through digital innovation
 

Applications Close: 11:59 pm, Wednesday 25 March 2026

Working at eHealth
Our people are at the heart of who we are. We support our people with great benefits so they can enrich health in millions of ways every day:
 

Flexible work options
Professional growth and career development

Various health and wellbeing programs to support our team

About the Opportunity
Join eHealth as a Senior Functional Oracle EPM Consultant of our high-performing Enterprise Performance Management (EPM) team. This role plays a pivotal part in driving operational excellence by aligning business strategy with performance insights, empowering data-driven decision-making across the organisation.
As a key member of the EPM team, you will lead the design, configuration, and optimisation of Oracle EPM solutions, collaborating closely with finance, business, and IT stakeholders to deliver capabilities in planning, forecasting and reporting.
This is more than just a systems role - it’s a strategic leadership opportunity to shape how our business executes its goals with precision, efficiency, and long-term sustainability.
 

In this role, you will:

Lead the functional design, configuration, testing, and deployment of Oracle EPM solutions.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to capture business requirements and translate them into scalable EPM solutions.
Support key enterprise planning activities including financial, workforce, capital, cash flow and operational planning, as well as consolidation, reporting, and reconciliation.
Guide project teams from requirements gathering through to post-implementation support.
Identify opportunities to optimise performance reporting and streamline processes using EPM tools.
Serve as a subject matter expert, advising senior leadership and project teams on EPM best practices.
Provide leadership across EPM initiatives, ensuring solutions are robust, scalable, sustainable, and aligned with business objectives.

 
This role is internally referred to as Senior Digital Specialist - ERP.
 
About you
We are looking for someone who has:
 
Extensive functional experience with Oracle EPM (Cloud and/or Hyperion) in both project delivery and support roles, with strong understanding of EPM architecture, data flows, and metadata management.
Proven delivery of end to end EPM implementations, from requirements gathering through design, configuration, testing, deployment, and support, across at least two modules such as Financial, Workforce, Capital or Cash Flow Planning, ARCS, PCMCS, Narrative Reporting, or EDMCS.
Deep knowledge of financial, workforce, and capital planning, consolidation, and management reporting, underpinned by strong core accounting principles.
Demonstrated ability to translate complex business requirements into scalable EPM solutions, aligning stakeholders on process, system, and change impacts.
Strong experience working within Agile and/or Waterfall environments, with sound understanding of project governance, DevOps practices, and delivery controls.
Proven leadership capability, including leading functional teams, reviewing solution designs, mentoring junior staff, and ensuring quality across deliverables.
Excellent communication and delivery skills, including producing high quality project documentation and training materials, managing project planning, estimation, risk, and progress, and effectively engaging stakeholders from operational teams to senior executives.

Deep knowledge of financial, workforce, and capital planning, consolidation, and management reporting, underpinned by strong core accounting principles or business acumen.

 
Need more information?
Position Description

How to apply

Share with us your cover letter and most up to date resume (up to 5 pages), highlighting your relevant skills and experience.  


We recognise that AI tools are increasingly being used to assist with resumes and applications. While we accept their use, we're eager to see your personality and get to know the person behind the application!


For further discussions around the role or recruitment process, including adjustments, please contact our Talent Advisor Olivia.liu@health.nsw.gov.au or Hiring Manager, Ken Chan at on Ken.Chan@health.nsw.gov.au 


We will review all applications after the closing date. If your application is successful, you may be invited to take part in additional assessments as part of the recruitment process.
 

Additional information
This recruitment may be used to create a Talent Pool for similar future roles (ongoing or temporary) that may arise over the next 18 months.
To be eligible for this role you must have current Australian work rights (Australian citizen, permanent resident, New Zealand citizen with a current passport, or hold a valid visa with permission to work in Australia).
If you currently reside outside NSW, please confirm in your application your willingness to relocate if successful.

Job Title: Principal Analyst – Data & Analytics Consulting | $200k+ Potential
Ad Text:
Build your own consulting team | High performance role | $200k+ potential

Location: Perth WA
Company: Gemcutter Analytics

Gemcutter Analytics is a fast-growing data analytics consultancy helping mining, construction and industrial businesses make better decisions through Power BI dashboards and business analytics.

We are looking for a Principal Analyst to lead client engagements and help build the next stage of our firm.

This is not a traditional corporate analytics job.

It is designed for someone who wants ownership, responsibility and significant upside.

The role offers a competitive base salary plus a high profit share structure, giving high performers the ability to earn $200k+ per year while building their own portfolio of clients and delivery team.

If you are looking for a comfortable internal analytics role with minimal commercial responsibility, this probably isn’t the role for you.

But if you are ambitious, commercially minded, and want the opportunity to build something meaningful, this could be a career-defining opportunity.







The Opportunity

As a Principal Analyst, you will manage a portfolio of client engagements and lead the delivery of analytics solutions that drive real business impact.

Our work focuses primarily on building dashboards and data models that help executive teams understand the drivers of their business.

You will act as a trusted advisor to senior leaders, while leading internal delivery teams to ensure projects are delivered to a high standard.

You will have the opportunity to build and lead your own team within Gemcutter Analytics, creating a “business within a business", where you'll be responsible for your own PnL.







What You'll Be Responsible For

Managing Client Relationships

Acting as the primary relationship manager for key client accounts

Understanding each client’s business drivers and strategic goals

Ensuring clients receive exceptional service and tangible value from analytics

Leading Analytics Projects

Scoping projects and defining deliverables with clients

Leading delivery teams building dashboards and analytics solutions

Ensuring work is delivered on time and to a high standard

Maintaining strong project economics and margins

Growing Existing Client Accounts

Identifying opportunities to expand work with existing clients

Supporting proposal development and client discussions

Contributing to the commercial growth of the firm

You will not be responsible for cold lead generation, but you will lead the proposal process and development of opportunities with existing clients.

Building and Leading a Team

Mentoring Associate and Senior Analysts

Supporting hiring as the team grows

Developing scalable delivery processes

Building a high-performing analytics team around you







Who This Role Is For

This role suits someone who is ready for the next level but feels limited in a traditional corporate environment.

You may currently be a:

Senior Analytics Consultant

Analytics Manager

BI Lead

Data & Analytics Manager

Consulting Manager

You’re likely someone who:

Wants more ownership and responsibility

Enjoys working directly with clients and solving business problems

Has strong commercial instincts

Is motivated by performance-based rewards

Wants to help build and grow a business







Technical Experience

You will have strong experience in:

Power BI (including DAX and data modelling)

Data transformation and modelling

Building dashboards for operational or financial performance

Working with complex business datasets

Translating business questions into analytical solutions

Experience with tools such as Fabric, Dataverse, KNIME or similar platforms is beneficial.







Compensation

This role is structured to reward performance and commercial success.

Competitive base salary plus a high profit share structure

Total earnings potential $200k+ per year

Opportunity to build and lead your own consulting team

Significant autonomy and influence within the firm







About Gemcutter Analytics

Gemcutter Analytics specialises in helping mid-sized companies unlock the value of their data.

We work closely with leadership teams to build dashboards and analytics that provide visibility into the drivers of their business.

Our clients operate across industries, including:

Mining

Construction

Industrial services

Equipment hire

Manufacturing

Our mission is simple: empower leaders with data.

Apply

If you’re an ambitious analytics professional looking for ownership, responsibility and real upside, we’d love to hear from you.

Job Title: Senior Power BI Visualisation Developer – Long-Term Daily rate Contract
Ad Text:
Join a growing Data & Analytics team and play a key role in a major reporting and data platform transformation, designing impactful Power BI dashboards used by stakeholders across the business.

We’re looking for a Senior Power BI Visualisation Developer to deliver high-impact dashboards and reports that turn complex data into clear, actionable insights. This is a visible, greenfield opportunity within a growing data function, helping modernise reporting capability and uplift analytics across the organisation.

What you’ll do:

Build Power BI dashboards and visualisations stakeholders rely on for decision making

Translate business requirements and KPIs into intuitive, actionable insights

Work closely with data engineers and analytics teams to leverage enterprise data models

Support modernisation and rationalisation of reporting across the business

Ensure dashboards are accurate, scalable, and aligned with governance standards

Engage with stakeholders to drive adoption and literacy of reporting tools

What we’re looking for:

Strong experience developing Power BI dashboards and visualisations

Solid SQL and data modelling skills

Experience with enterprise data platforms or data warehouses

Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Passion for data storytelling and visualisation best practices

Why this role is exciting:

Greenfield reporting transformation – shape reporting from the ground up

High visibility across the business

Influence reporting standards and modern analytics practices

Join a collaborative, growing AI & Data team with career progression opportunities

Long-term, chunky contract with a competitive daily rate

Ready to take your Power BI career to the next level? Apply now and help shape the future of reporting and analytics!

Location: Sydney preferred, however open to candidates across Australia. Candidates must have full Australian working rights.

Responsibilities:

Conduct thorough testing of data models, transformations, and reporting layers to ensure accuracy and reliability.
Validate data integrity within Data Warehouses and Data Lakes using structured testing techniques.
Perform functional and regression testing of reports and data extracts to meet business requirements.
Collaborate with analysts and developers to ensure data models accurately reflect business needs.
Use SQL and modern data tools to troubleshoot issues, support ETL testing, and enhance automation.

Requirements:

Demonstrated expertise in testing data models and transformations within analytics and reporting environments.
Strong experience in validating Data Warehouses and Data Lakes using structured schematic models.
Proven ability to test and verify reports and dashboards developed in Microsoft Power BI.
Solid knowledge of ETL processes and tools; experience with Microsoft Fabric and Microsoft Dynamics 365 is a plus.
Advanced proficiency in SQL, particularly when working with Microsoft SQL Server.

If you think this job opportunity fits what you are looking for my client would like to arrange interviews as soon as possible.

a.oberzeiser@nigelfrank.com


Your benefits of working with Nigel Frank:

Nigel Frank International is the world's leading executive search firm specializing in the recruitment of exclusively Microsoft Dynamics (AX, NAV& CRM) and Microsoft Technologies professional and managerial staff.

We have the largest job offering in our specialty area of MS Dynamics and the Microsoft Stack. We have quickly become the leader in Microsoft Dynamics recruitment, offering well over one thousand open jobs in Microsoft Dynamics and the Microsoft Stack.

The Opportunity
  
Elysium Digital is expanding its AI consulting practice, and we’re looking for an experienced and forward-thinking consultant to help shape and lead our work in this exciting and fast-evolving space. As an AI Strategy & Enablement Consultant, you’ll work with public and private sector clients to design and deliver impactful strategies, frameworks and pilots that enable the responsible and effective adoption of artificial intelligence.
  
This is a hands-on consulting role with strong growth potential. You’ll support client engagements across AI readiness, prototyping, governance, enablement and architecture, while also contributing to the development of new offerings, IP and capability within the firm. If you're a curious and confident consultant with a passion for AI, this is a rare opportunity to help shape how organisations transform with emerging technologies.
  
What Will You Do?
  
As an AI Strategy & Enablement Consultant, your responsibilities will include:

Client delivery: Plan, lead and support AI-related consulting engagements ranging from strategy development and readiness assessments through to design sprints, prototyping and governance frameworks.
Workshop facilitation: Design and run collaborative workshops, to explore opportunities and build client capability.
Strategy and reporting: Develop high-quality deliverables including strategy documents, assessment reports, roadmaps, governance models and executive briefings.
Business development: Contribute to proposals, RFQs and client discussions to shape new opportunities and extend Elysium’s AI practice.
Practice and offering development: Help shape and expand Elysium’s suite of AI service offerings, building tools, frameworks, training content, and accelerators.
Client engagement: Build trusted relationships with client stakeholders, offering clear, pragmatic advice informed by both business context and technology trends.
Cross-functional collaboration: Work closely with colleagues across strategy, design, architecture, data and delivery to bring multidisciplinary perspectives to client problems.
  
What Makes You a Good Fit?
  
We’re looking for someone who:

Has solid experience as a management consultant, enterprise or solution architect, senior business analyst, or similar role in the strategy and advisory space.
Is naturally curious and has a strong interest in artificial intelligence – especially generative AI – and how it can be applied in government and enterprise contexts.
Has strong consulting skills and is able to facilitate workshops, write clearly, structure strategic advice, and communicate with influence.
Can work end-to-end across engagements: shaping opportunities, planning activities, delivering value, and supporting clients through ambiguity.
Is comfortable with both business and technology domains, and able to bridge between them.
Is self-motivated and proactive, excited to build out a new practice area and shape what comes next.
Holds or is eligible for an AGSVA security clearance.
  
You don’t need to be a data scientist or AI engineer, but you should have a solid understanding of how AI works, where it adds value, and the challenges of adopting it responsibly in large organisations.
  
Why Join Elysium Digital?
  
At Elysium Digital, we bring together experienced strategists, designers, technologists and engineers to help our clients solve complex challenges and deliver better digital outcomes. You’ll benefit from:

Meaningful work: Tackle high-impact problems in government and industry, with a focus on emerging technologies and responsible innovation.
Supportive team culture: Be part of a tight-knit Strategy & Advisory practice that values trust, collaboration and professional growth.
Professional development: Access structured pathways to grow your consulting skills and shape your own career trajectory.
Hybrid working model: Flexibility to work from home, client sites, and our Barton office in Canberra. Candidates from other capital cities are welcome.
Creative freedom: Help build a new practice area from the ground up, with room to bring ideas to life and make your mark.
  
Interested?
If you're excited by the intersection of AI, strategy and human-centred consulting, we’d love to hear from you.
  
Apply now by sending your CV and a short cover letter to daniel.farrell@elysiumdigital.com.au or apply here

At LeavePlus, our mission is to support and protect the wellbeing of construction workers by ensuring they receive the portable leave entitlements they have earned. This Delivery Manager opportunity is a unique opportunity for a strategic and delivery-focused leader to play a pivotal role in the next chapter of our transformation.

As our Strategic Portfolio Delivery Manager, you will sit at the heart of our Enterprise Project Management Office (EPMO), partnering closely with the EPMO Manager and senior leaders to drive the delivery of LeavePlus' most important strategic initiatives. You will oversee a portfolio of projects that directly enable our business plan and strategy, ensuring initiatives are delivered with discipline, clarity and measurable impact.

This role will suit a high-calibre thought leader who enjoys influencing strategy while ensuring meaningful delivery, offering the rare opportunity to drive enterprise-level change with social impact.

If you are energised by leading complex portfolios, building high-performing delivery frameworks and making a tangible impact on an industry that matters the please review the key elements of the role below:

What you'll do

Lead portfolio governance: Establish clear governance frameworks that align projects with organisational priorities and enable confident, informed decision-making across the enterprise.

Oversee strategic delivery: Guide and support project teams to ensure initiatives are delivered on time, within scope and budget, and aligned to business outcomes.

Monitor performance: Track portfolio health through KPIs, milestones and reporting dashboards that provide clear visibility of progress and impact.

Manage risk and resources: Proactively identify risks or delivery bottlenecks and work across teams to keep projects moving forward.

Drive consistency and improvement: Continuously refine project management frameworks and processes to strengthen delivery capability across the organisation in a fast-paced environment.

Enable collaboration and capability: Foster strong cross-functional partnerships and mentor business owners, equipping them with the tools and support needed to deliver successful outcomes.

Embed new ways of working: Design and implement practical frameworks and methodologies that create consistent, sustainable ways of working across LeavePlus.

What you'll bring

Big picture insights and translation of strategic thinking into project action planning

An accurate and sharp mind

Solution focused mindset with proactive risk identification

Advanced cross-functional stakeholder management

An outcome-driven nature

A passion for guiding and mentoring championing and fostering growth across the team

What you'll need to have:

Complex project portfolio delivery experience, proven examples of initiatives delivered on time, within scope and aligned to organisational-wide strategy.

Evidence of strong capabilities across governance, risk and assurance, establishing frameworks and mitigating portfolio risks

Demonstrated experience developing performance monitoring tools, dashboards and executive reporting to track portfolio health and outcomes.

Successful examples displaying resource and capacity management, optimising allocation across multiple projects.

Familiarity with strong cross-functional leadership and collaboration, aligning project delivery with business analysis and change management activities.

Proven track record of improving and standardising project management processes to drive consistency and operational efficiency.

These qualifications are desirable:

Bachelor’s Degree: In Business Administration, Project Management, Finance

Professional Certifications: PMP (Project Management Professional), PgMP (Program Management Professional), or equivalent certifications in project and portfolio management

Agile Certification: Certified ScrumMaster (CSM), PMI-ACP (Agile Certified Practitioner), or equivalent Agile certification.

Worked with Jira

To apply, please submit your tailored CV and cover letter addressing how your relevant experience aligns to the above role criteria and why you're an ideal fit for this senior opportunity and LeavePlus. We look forward reviewing your interest.

About the role 


The purpose of the Renewal Pricing Manager role will be to lead the existing customer pricing strategy and pricing assurance function. This includes managing renewal pricing algorithms and processes. To be successful in this role, the Pricing Manager should have solid technical skills and an ability to lead a technical team. The Pricing Manager will help the Pricing Team manage its key processes to ensure ongoing profitability while minimising financial risks.

Manage the end-to-end renewal pricing process, including data, modelling, review, implementation, and monitoring
Consider and evaluate alternative data sources and approaches for renewal pricing

High-impact analytical role supporting tenders, pricing and commercial decisions

Exposure to senior stakeholders in a complex, capital-intensive business

At Dyno Nobel Limited our people vision is to unlock our people capability and culture to create safe, inclusive and high performing teams on the ground.

The Role

As a Commercial Analyst, you will support the commercial team by delivering high-quality financial analysis, modelling and reporting that underpins key commercial and strategic decisions. This is a newly created role, offering strong exposure to tenders, pricing activity and stakeholder engagement across a complex and fast-paced organisation.

Reporting to the Commercial leadership team, you will play a hands-on role in generating and maintaining financial models, producing recurring reports and supporting ad hoc analysis to help the business understand performance, manage risk and drive value.

Key responsibilities include:

·         Building and maintaining financial models to support tenders, pricing and commercial decisions

·         Generating and maintaining recurring reports (daily, weekly and monthly)

·         Supporting cost build-ups, margin analysis and validation of commercial assumptions

·         Assisting with tender activity, including analytical support during peak periods

·         Producing clear, accurate analysis to support decision-making across the commercial and finance teams

·         Managing multiple priorities while maintaining strong attention to detail and data accuracy




What you’ll bring

·         A strong commitment to positively contributing to our safe, inclusive and high-performance culture

·         Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Finance, Economics & Engineering

·         Experience in an analytical, commercial or accounting-focused role

·         Strong analytical and problem-solving capability with high attention to detail

·         Advanced Excel skills and experience working with financial models

·         Experience with SAP, Power BI and the ability to present insights clearly (including at senior stakeholder level)




If you are excited about the role and think you have what it takes but your experience doesn’t align 100% we still want to hear from you.




Benefits to you

·         We are a team focused on safety, inclusion and high performance

·         18 weeks paid parental leave for primary carer, 3 weeks for secondary carer

·         Superannuation paid on unpaid parental leave, up to 12 months

·         Hybrid work model

·         Salary packaging options

·         Confidential employee assistance program

·         Mental Health and Well-being program

·         Options to purchase annual leave (where eligible)

·         Various corporate discount programs

·         Workplace giving charitable scheme

·         Opportunities for career progression through succession planning and development reviews




Please be advised that our hybrid work model entails three office days and two remote workdays per week. This structure aims to enhance efficiency and teamwork, while upholding a harmonious work flexibility.

As the National Manager - Finance Business Partner based in Canberra, you will be responsible for guiding the organisation's financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and performance analysis.
You will provide expert financial insights to senior leaders, ensuring alignment between financial outcomes and organisational priorities, while driving strong governance and supporting informed, data-driven decision-making across the business.

Details about the Client

ADF Careers is the largest workforce outsourcing solution in the southern hemisphere with over 80,000 applications received on average each year.
ADF Careers is on a mission to recruit the right people, in the right numbers, at the right time to support the Australian Defence Force to build, sustain and maximise Defence capability.


Our people are our greatest asset. We recognise and respect our people's need to work in ways that support their careers, families, and lifestyle. That's why we offer flexible working arrangements, learning pathways, additional leave entitlements and extensive corporate discounts across a variety of product and services. We want our people to be financially rewarded; to feel good and to go beyond what they thought possible at work.

Be you, and we'll be better together.

To find out more about our great employee benefits and what it's like to be part of the team, reach out to our friendly talent acquisition team.

Job Description

The National Manager - Finance Business Partner is a strategic leadership role responsible for leading the organisation's financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, pricing and performance analysis team. This role works closely with senior leaders and provides strategic insight and analytical leadership to inform decision-making strengthen financial governance and drive business performance. Based at our Headquarters in Canberra, this role is the first point of contact for all Finance Business Partner matters, providing expert advice to our Senior Leadership Team and supporting the Head of Finance to ensure alignment between financial outcomes and organisational priorities.

Job Description

About the Role

At SEEK, our Senior Data Scientists play a key role in shaping how millions of people discover meaningful work. As a Senior Data Scientist in the Personalisation team, you’ll lead the design and delivery of end-to-end AI solutions—from defining problems and running experiments to building models and deploying them at scale.

You’ll work on some of SEEK’s most impactful challenges, including developing deep candidate representations, personalising user experiences, and powering adaptive content across our platforms. You’ll collaborate closely with AI Engineers, Product Managers, Designers, and other Data Scientists, while also mentoring and supporting less experienced team members.

This role is perfect for someone who thrives in autonomy, enjoys solving ambiguous problems, and is passionate about creating high-impact, technically excellent solutions. If you’re excited about driving innovation and influencing the direction of AI at SEEK, we’d love to hear from you.

The Team

The Personalisation team sits within SEEK’s Artificial Intelligence & Analytics (AIPS) group and plays a critical role in shaping how candidates interact with our products. We help users find the right opportunities faster by tailoring their experiences using behavioural signals, advanced machine learning, and deep user insights.

As part of a cross-functional, specialised team, we design and deploy models that power intelligent content systems and personalisation engines. We work in quarterly delivery cycles where we explore, prototype, and release solutions that enhance the job discovery experience for millions.

What You’ll Do

In this role, you’ll lead end-to-end personalisation initiatives—working closely with Product Managers, Engineers, and other Data Scientists to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning solutions. You’ll take ownership of the technical direction, shaping the roadmap and driving experimentation to solve complex user experience challenges.

You’ll develop and refine ML models, run rigorous evaluations including A/B tests, and ensure performance, fairness, and reliability in production. You’ll also uncover data-driven insights that influence product strategy, while providing mentorship, technical guidance, and thought leadership across the team.

Qualifications
8+ years’ experience in data science, machine learning, or applied AI, ideally in product-focused environments.
A track record of owning end-to-end ML solutions – from idea and experimentation through to robust, production deployment and iteration.
A strong theoretical foundation in machine learning, statistics, and data analysis, paired with a pragmatic approach to solving real-world problems.
A Masters or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related quantitative discipline.
Fluency in Python and SQL, and hands-on experience with modern data science tooling (e.g. Pandas, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch).
Practical experience with deep learning, ranking models, recommendation systems, or personalisation frameworks used at scale.
Deep experience in experimentation – designing and running A/B tests, interpreting results, and applying rigorous model evaluation techniques to drive continuous improvement.
Additional Information

Permanent Perks
At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners

VALD is the world leader in technology for the allied health industry, providing innovative human-measurement technology to over  8,000 clients in over 150 countries. If you have a favorite team in the NBA, EPL, or NFL, there's a good chance they use VALD Technologies.

Since its humble beginnings in 2015 in Brisbane, Australia, VALD has grown to a team of over 300 team members in over 30 countries, with 5 offices across four continents. 

Driven by a multidisciplinary team of researchers, clinicians, sports scientists, designers, developers and engineers, VALD’s suite of systems offer unparalleled insight into human movement, performance, injury risk and rehabilitation.

About the role

VALD is building the world’s largest musculoskeletal database, with over two billion data points and counting. Using this data, we can help practitioners to objectively assess patients’ progress and make more accurate, data-informed treatment decisions. We are looking for an experienced data scientist to join our growing team and build the technical foundations for VALD’s future growth.

In this role, you will: 

Lead improvements in VALD’s normative data generation processes, further enabling practitioners to easily benchmark patients’ performance against a reference group. 

Develop, test and deploy machine learning models for applications such as data quality improvements, rehabilitation trajectory modelling, and predictive device maintenance. 

Apply dimensional modelling expertise to further enhance the Gold layer of the VALD Lakehouse, improving how data products are served to clients. 

Conduct ad-hoc data analysis to support various initiatives across VALD, including patient rehabilitation analytics and customized normative data reporting. 

Contribute to and maintain the data science codebase, implementing best practices in development life cycle management and source control. 

Is this you?

Our ideal candidate has: 

Tertiary qualifications in mathematics, statistics, engineering, or a related field. 
Proficiency in Python (including PySpark and ML libraries), Databricks, SQL, Git, R, Excel, Power BI and R.
Proven experience building machine learning models in Python. 
Expertise in delivering dimensional data models using Kimbal principles. 
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure and its application in data science. 
All the attributes that make a great data scientist – curiosity, attention to detail, teamwork, communication skills, a passion for problem-solving, and a willingness to meet deadlines. 
Ability to handle ambiguity and thrive in a startup environment. 

Why VALD?

An opportunity to be part of revolutionizing the health and performance industries is just the beginning when you join VALD.  

We have a range of benefits we offer to our team, such as:

The opportunity to work in a company that is redefining allied healthcare.
Working with a range of high-performing individuals and teams across a range of disciplines. 
Working with contemporary tooling on industry first applications. 
Casual work attire.
Chefs that prepare you delicious fresh breakfast and lunch.
On-site barista to make your coffee just as you like it.
Team gym, masseuse, and access to physiotherapy and exercise physiology services.

Diversity & Inclusion Commitment

VALD’s best asset is not our technology but our people and culture. A culture of inclusion and diversity is critical to our business. We know diverse teams perform better. It's not a separate initiative – we aim to embed inclusion and diversity in everything we do. We are committed to fostering an inclusive work environment and embracing diversity, including gender, nationality, disability, age, marital/parental status, ethnicity, gender identity, socioeconomic background and sexual orientation. We welcome applications from people from all backgrounds.

Apply now

In your cover letter, please share with us why you think your experience is relevant to this role and how this opportunity fits into your longer-term career goals. We can't wait to meet you and discuss what a career with VALD could look like for you!

About Us    

At Development Victoria, we’re passionate about making Victoria a great place to be. We create vibrant places across our state, through diverse and strategic property development and urban renewal projects.    

As the Victorian Government’s development arm, we revitalise iconic public buildings, create important community, creative and sporting facilities and repurpose public land, for all Victorians. We also build great homes and communities where people and businesses can thrive, catering for different budgets, lifestyles, and people - designed to be sustainable and affordable.    




Your New Role

As the IT Functional Analyst, you’ll ensure the successful implementation of solutions within Development Victoria’s application ecosystem by using your advanced facilitation skills, encouraging collaboration and innovation, with a strong focus on end-user experience and delivering business value.

You’ll report to a manager with more than 20 years of experience in the IT industry, specialising in project delivery and program management. Under her leadership, you’ll gain exposure to policy and process development, change management principles, and established project delivery frameworks. She is a collaborative and people‑focused leader who empowers her team to work autonomously while ensuring they feel supported, connected, and set up for success.

This role is an ongoing, hybrid opportunity, with a mix of working at our head office in Melbourne CBD and working from home - we work flexibly at Development Victoria.

Core Accountabilities: 

 

Understanding, analysing and documenting current business processes, identifying areas for improvement, and mapping them to software functionalities.
Collaborating with stakeholders to define and document business requirements to gain acceptance and agreement, ensuring clarity and alignment with project objectives.
Developing functional specifications and designing solutions that meet business requirements.
Configuring and customising application solutions to align with business requirements, ensuring optimal performance and user experience.
Creating and executing test plans, ensuring that the implemented solution meets the defined requirements and functions as expected.
Providing transitional support to users on implemented solutions to ensure smooth adoption and usage.
Contributing to the development of training to team members and end-users which may involve collaborating with change leads to identify needs.
Provide support to IT projects in the areas of evaluation, options assessment and business-case development.
Coordinating, participating in and facilitating activities such as workshops, project meetings, stand-ups, vendor evaluations, user feedback analysis.
Participating in project planning, tracking progress, reporting and managing tasks to ensure timely delivery of solutions. 
Supporting change management activities by clearly communicating updates, upgrades, and enhancements to stakeholders.
Communicating with stakeholders at all levels, translating technical information for business users and vice versa.
Collaborating with solution providers and external parties to ensure solutions are delivered per agreed requirements and timelines, escalating issues as required.



About You

There is a big focus on bridging business requirements and technical solutions in this role, so you will need to be someone that is comfortable in and have experience with facilitating workshops, determining business needs and requirements and a solid understanding of technical language. You have experience with taking technical language and being able to interpret it into something that the business can understand. You’re a clear written & verbal communicator who can collaborate well with others and deal with a variety of stakeholders, both internally and externally.

Employee Benefits    

We drive strong collaboration and support our teams to work virtually and flexibly.    
Professional development opportunities available including;     
Study support    
Mentoring programs    
Opportunity to participate in cross functional working groups that drive diversity and inclusion and sustainability initiatives.    
Novated leasing    
Purchase leave    
Paid parental leave    
Technology salary sacrifice    
Employee referral bonuses     
Health & Wellbeing initiatives such as EAP and mental health and wellbeing leave    
Access to group life & group salary continuance insurance    
Modern onsite facilities

How will you make an impact?

As an Investment Analyst you will play an important role in contributing towards delivering strong investment returns to our members by providing high quality investment research and advice in line with our investment strategy.

The opportunity

We are seeking a broad-based Investment Analyst to join our dynamic Investment Strategy team. This position will play an important role in supporting the Head of Investment Research and Portfolio Managers across asset classes. You will be responsible for delivering strong investment returns to our members by providing high-quality investment research on investment managers, markets, asset allocation, foreign exchange, and/or thematics in line with our investment strategy.

In this role, you will perform quantitative and qualitative analysis of investment opportunities across asset classes, support ongoing monitoring of existing investments, and undertake research projects on investment-related thematics, sectors, and markets. You will also prepare regular investment reports and presentations for stakeholders, collaborate with the investment team to develop investment strategies, and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and internal policies. To be successful in this role, you will be curious, inquisitive, and have a passion for investments.

Investment generalists or those yet to settle on an area of specialisation are encouraged to apply.

About you

You will have a minimum of 3 years of experience in investment analysis or a related field, with strong analytical skills and knowledge of financial markets and investments. Your strong written and presentation abilities will enable you to communicate effectively across all organisational levels. You will have the ability to apply deep research to conceptualise and develop solutions, and you will demonstrate curiosity, contrarian & lateral thought, integrity, and a desire for ongoing and continual improvement at both the personal and organisational levels.

You will hold tertiary qualifications in a relevant discipline such as science, economics, engineering, finance, or investment management. Additionally, CFA, CA, CPA, or CIMA qualifications are preferred.

What can we offer you?

Our employees are passionate about what we do and are proud to be part of an organisation which helps everyday Australians achieve a brighter future. We value diversity of thought and have an open and flexible workplace. Some industry leading benefits we offer include:

Hybrid and flexible work environment
15% superannuation
Additional paid leave over Christmas and New Year
Additional 5 days paid “flex leave”, so that you can take time out when you need it
18 weeks paid parental leave
3 days community service leave
4 weeks paid gender affirmation leave
Access to long service leave at 5 years
Access to subsidised financial advice
Dedicated per person development budget to support your growth

Just to name a few!

About us

At Hostplus, we passionately provide our over 1.7 million members with the best possible service; constantly looking for new ways to deliver better value while helping members get the most out of their super. We are inspired to help our members achieve greater security for their future and ensure they retire with dignity.

Your role at Hostplus will make a difference, whether you're in marketing, financial advice, operations or any of our other divisions - you'll be contributing to ensuring our members retire with the best possible outcomes, and what better feeling is there than knowing your role helps someone to retire with dignity and security?

Our commitment to diversity and inclusion

We are passionate about creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity. We believe diversity of thought, background and experience strengthens relationships and delivers meaningful benefits to our people, our members, and the communities we operate in.

We welcome and encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, ages, religions, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, LGBTIQ+ people, those with a disability, neurodiverse individuals, women, and people with a cultural or linguistically diverse background. Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
We are exclusively partnering with our client on a unique, senior-level management opportunity within their Accounting and Tax division that stands out in the ANZ market right now.

Our client is a highly regarded global business management software provider supporting 60,000+ customers across sectors including hospitality, manufacturing, not-for-profit, construction and more. Within the Accounting and Tax division specifically, there are 8,000 customers already in play in ANZ - creating a significant embedded base for cross-sell, innovation, and commercial expansion.

What's particularly compelling is the level of investment. The business is investing heavily in developing new products, including Gen AI-powered capabilities, with the clear ambition of leapfrogging competitors in the region. This isn't incremental improvement - it's about redefining how accounting professionals work through intelligent automation, workflow optimisation, and data-driven insights.

Our client continues to gain market share through both strong organic growth and strategic acquisitions, and the Accounting and Tax portfolio is a key strategic pillar. This role will be highly visible and well-supported by senior leadership and the CEO - you'll have genuine backing to shape direction, influence investment decisions, and execute at scale.

From a role perspective, this is a true strategic product leadership opportunity:

You'll define and communicate the product vision and strategy, owning the end-to-end lifecycle from ideation through to launch and optimisation.
You'll translate complex accounting workflows and regulatory requirements into intuitive, high-impact product features.
You'll leverage deep customer research, competitive analysis, usage data, and industry trends to shape differentiated roadmaps.
You'll drive profitability through integrated solutions, pricing and packaging optimisation, and strong go-to-market collaboration with Sales and Marketing.
You'll manage and mentor a team of 11, elevating the product practice, fostering a culture of experimentation and customer-centricity.


It's a highly commercial, customer-facing, and cross-functional role - working closely with Engineering, UX, Customer Success and executive stakeholders in a dynamic, high-growth environment.

While the role is ANZ-based and focused primarily on regional execution, there's also exposure (circa 15%) to broader European and global growth strategy - giving you international influence without diluting your core mandate.

For someone with deep B2B SaaS experience, ideally within accounting, financial services or tax technology, and a track record of leading product teams through growth and transformation, this represents a rare opportunity:

Scale and established market presence
Heavy investment in AI and innovation
Strong acquisition and organic growth momentum
Executive-level visibility and support
Clear opportunity to shape the future of accounting software in the region


If you're looking for a role where you can combine strategic vision, commercial impact, and innovation leadership - this is absolutely worth a confidential conversation.

Ad Text:
Senior Adviser Finance - Finance Business Partnering, Bauxite, Alumina and Pacific Corporate Operations


Key finance business partner role in a diverse and global organisation:

Advance your career in a dynamic, supportive team environment
Many other additional benefits including flexible working, Rio Tinto share scheme, Rio Discounts Program, contribution to medical costs, leave loading and more!


About the role


Finding better ways to provide the materials the world needs.


Rio Tinto's Weipa operations in Far North QLD, are on the look-out for curious minded individuals who want to take on a rewarding career with us! 2026 is set to be an exciting year for our teams, with activities across a range of site-based initiatives for both people and processes.


We are looking for a Senior Adviser - Finance Business Partnering to be part of a high performing finance team, based in Brisbane with travel to Weipa.

The finance team is committed to providing professional support in understanding, interpreting and communicating business financial performance.

This role will work with multiple stakeholders to provide advice to the operations teams and support the development and execution of strategic decision making.


We are an open, connected global team that includes some of the industry's best and brightest minds. We offer competitive, performance-focused remuneration and a wide range of benefits to reward your contribution. With a global reach, the opportunities to develop and grow your career are broad and exciting.


Reporting to the Manager Finance at Weipa and working in a challenging and exciting environment within the Finance team, you will be:

Responsible for site planning, forecasting and variance reporting.
Interpret monthly, quarterly and annual financial results.
Support Business analysis, including valuation of projects and strategic financial modelling for validation of various business opportunities
Provide support across site and partner with stakeholders to proactively drive and influence financial improvement through analysis and cost leadership deployment
Provide financial training to all levels of the organization to enhance financial acumen
Work with the global finance function teams to implement fit for purpose systems, processes, metrics and reporting
Identify financial opportunities and influence outcomes to enhance the performance of the business
Maintains effective reporting mechanisms to ensure internal stakeholders are provided with insightful and appropriate analysis of financial performance.
Provides analyses to internal stakeholders on all matters of potentially significant financial impact to the company, including economic and competitive trends, pricing, cash flow, product line evaluation and capital additions.
Interprets monthly, quarterly and annual financial results for the assigned perimeter (e.g. Business Unit).


What you'll bring


To be successfully considered for this role we are looking for:

Bachelor and/or professional qualification e.g. CA, CPA, CIMA.
Advanced excel skills
Power BI skills
Management accounting or business analysis background
Strong stakeholder engagement skills
Team orientated approach to delivery of strategic priorities
Proven experience in identifying financial opportunities
Effective presentation skills and ability to present strategic information


It will also be beneficial if you have:

Experience in use of SAP and the Rio Tinto Business Solution (specifically PAM and MMP)
Proficiency with business intelligence programs/techniques (Power BI, EasyMorph and SQL)


This role provides:

A work environment where safety is always the number one priority 
A permanent position working directly for Rio Tinto 
A competitive base salary reflective of your skills and experience with annual incentive program 
Comprehensive medical benefits including subsidised private health insurance for employees and immediate family 
Be recognized for your contribution, your thinking and your hard work, and go home knowing you've helped the world progress.
Attractive share ownership plan 
Company provided insurance cover 
Extensive salary sacrifice & salary packaging options 
Career development & education assistance to further your technical or leadership ambitions 
Ongoing access to family-friendly health and medical wellbeing support 
Leave for all of life's reasons (vacation/annual, paid parental, sick leave, cultural leave)
To better suit different family needs, our paid parental leave approach gives all parents the option to take 18 weeks of paid leave after a new child arrives - at a time that suits them
Exclusive employee discounts including hotel stays, banking, accommodation, cars, retail and more


Where you will be working


The role is based in Brisbane with travel to Rio Tinto Weipa (and working directly at Amrun) also required. Weipa is located 900km north-west of Cairns on the Gulf of Carpentaria and has been operating for over 50 years.

#gts

About Rio Tinto

Rio Tinto is a leading global mining and materials company. We operate in 35 countries where we produce iron ore, copper, aluminium, critical minerals, and other materials needed for the global energy transition and for people, communities, and nations to thrive.

We have been mining for 150 years and operate with knowledge built up across generations and continents. Our purpose is finding better ways to provide the materials the world needs - striving for innovation and continuous improvement to produce materials with low emissions and to the right environmental, social and governance standards. But we can't do it on our own, so we're focused on creating partnerships to solve problems, create win-win situations and meet opportunities.

Respect and Inclusion

At Rio Tinto, we particularly welcome and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, women, the LGBTI+ community, mature workers, people with disabilities and people from different cultural backgrounds.

We are committed to an inclusive environment where people feel comfortable to be themselves. We want our people to feel that all voices are heard, all cultures respected and that a variety of perspectives are not only welcome - they are essential to our success. We treat each other fairly and with dignity regardless of race, gender, nationality, ethnic origin, religion, age, sexual orientation or anything else that makes us different.

IT Project Manager with strong Business Analysis -Sydney/Anywhere in Australia

We are seeking a hands-on IT Project Manager with strong Business Analysis capability to support the early stages of several IT initiatives.

This role will focus on understanding current systems and processes, defining requirements, and preparing projects to move through governance and into delivery.

The role requires someone who is comfortable wearing both Project Manager and Business Analyst hats and working closely with business and technical stakeholders.

Requirements documentation

Support planning and prioritisation of initiatives aligned to the organisation’s IT roadmap

Work closely with internal teams to prepare projects for delivery readiness

8+ years experience in IT project delivery and/or business analysis

3+ years of experience in IT Project Management

Demonstrated experience supporting IT systems or digital transformation initiatives

Strong experience preparing project initiation artefacts and governance documentation

Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills

Ability to work independently in early-stage projects where scope may evolve

Ideal Candidate Profile

Background as Senior Business Analyst, Project Manager, or hybrid PM/BA

Experience working across systems delivery and process improvement initiatives

Comfortable working in hands-on roles in smaller or early-stage projects

Senior Business Analysts seeking to move into Project Management will be highly regarded

Senior Workday Business Analyst | HCM & Recruitment Lead

Location: Sydney (Hybrid - 3 days per week on-site)

Program: Large-Scale Workday Transformation

The Mission

We are looking for a powerhouse Senior Workday Business Analyst to join a flagship transformation program for one of Sydney’s leading tertiary institutions. This isn’t just a "business as usual" role—you will be the bridge between our internal stakeholders and our delivery partner, ensuring that the Core HCM, Recruitment, Absence, and Compensation modules are not just "implemented," but optimized for a complex, academic environment.

With a heavy emphasis on the Recruitment module, you will set the benchmark for documentation, process mapping, and functional excellence.

Your Impact

Module Ownership: Lead the functional analysis for Workday Recruitment, Core HCM, Absence, and Compensation.

Vendor Management: Partner closely with our implementation partner to ensure delivery hits the mark, challenging assumptions and maintaining high documentation standards.

Leadership: Provide mentorship and potentially direct supervision to a small team of BAs, ensuring a unified approach to requirements gathering.

Stakeholder Navigation: Translate complex Workday functionality into business value for academic and professional staff who may be new to the platform.

Process Excellence: Audit and improve the current state of documentation (BRDs, FDS, Process Maps) to ensure a seamless transition to the "To-Be" state.

The Technical Toolkit

The Non-Negotiables: You have a proven track record of delivering multiple end-to-end Workday implementations.

Functional Depth: Expert-level knowledge of Workday Recruitment is essential, complemented by strong proficiency in Core HCM, Absence, and Compensation.

Communication Mastery: You possess the "soft skills" required to manage diverse stakeholders in a complex, multi-layered environment like Higher Education.

Documentation Rigour: You take pride in creating clear, concise, and scalable functional specifications.

The "Cherry on Top" (Preferred)

Advanced Comp & Performance: Experience in Advanced Compensation and Performance Management modules.

Sector Experience: Prior experience within the Australian Higher Education sector (understanding the nuances of academic cycles and enterprise agreements).

Why Join This Program?

This is a high-visibility role within a major Sydney institution. You’ll have the autonomy to shape a small team, the challenge of a large-scale implementation, and the reward of seeing your work transform the employee experience for thousands of staff.

Job Title: Senior Governance, Risk and Compliance Analyst
Ad Text:
Senior Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) Analyst
Location: CBD, Sydney
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role An exciting opportunity exists for an experienced Senior Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) Analyst to join a high-performing technology-focused organisation. This role plays a critical part in strengthening governance, risk management, and compliance capabilities across the organisation.

Working closely with senior leadership, you will be responsible for driving governance and risk initiatives, supporting security and compliance frameworks, and ensuring alignment with industry standards and regulatory requirements. You will collaborate with technology, engineering, product, and business teams to design and implement effective controls, policies, and risk management practices.

Key Responsibilities
Provide subject matter expertise and guidance on governance, risk, and compliance matters across the organisation.
Support the technology strategy and roadmap by ensuring governance and compliance requirements are embedded in new initiatives and services.
Assist with the implementation and certification of ISO 42001:2023.
Maintain and enhance the Information Security Management System (ISMS) to support compliance with ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II.
Review and improve technology policies, standards, and procedures to address emerging security threats and evolving regulatory requirements.
Support the organisation’s risk management program, identifying potential risks across systems, applications, and infrastructure.
Coordinate risk mitigation activities in partnership with engineering, product, technology, and business teams.
Conduct vendor due diligence and third-party risk assessments.
Support internal and external audit processes and preparation activities.
Contribute to business impact analysis and maintain the enterprise risk register.
Perform compliance gap assessments and monitor developments in regulatory and cybersecurity landscapes.
Measures of Success
Ongoing review and improvement of ISMS policies, procedures, and governance documentation.
Effective implementation and maintenance of the organisation’s Risk Management Framework.
Timely completion of vendor due diligence and third-party risk assessments.
Successful preparation and participation in ISO27001, ISO42001, and SOC2 audits.
Contribution to the implementation of emerging AI governance and compliance standards.
Skills & Experience
Demonstrated experience in a Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) role within a technology or cybersecurity environment.
Experience implementing and maintaining security and compliance frameworks.
Strong knowledge of cybersecurity governance frameworks, regulations, and standards.
Solid understanding of cybersecurity principles, technologies, and risk management practices.
Experience managing risk registers, risk tooling, and reporting processes.
Strong experience supporting audit and certification processes.
Proven ability to lead organisational change initiatives related to compliance and governance.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to work independently while collaborating across multiple teams.
Frameworks & Standards Experience Experience working with several of the following frameworks is highly regarded:

ISO 27001
ISO 42001
SOC 2
NIST
CIS Controls
Essential Eight
GDPR
DORA
AI governance and security standards
Certifications (Desirable)
CISM
CISA
CISSP
ISO 27001 Lead Implementer or Lead Auditor
Why Apply?
This role offers the opportunity to work in a modern technology environment where governance, security, and compliance play a strategic role in business operations. You will collaborate with experienced professionals and contribute to strengthening enterprise risk and compliance capabilities in a growing organisation.

Job Description
Love what you do and leave your mark
Melbourne or Sydney location 
Own the Process. Drive the future.

We're looking for a highly capable Process Excellence Owner to take full accountability for the design, governance, and continuous improvement of one or more critical end-to-end enterprise processes.

You will hold the keys to our most critical end-to-end enterprise workflows. This is a high-impact role where you don't just follow a map you draw it. You will take accountability for the health, agility, and strategic direction of our core processes, ensuring they are not only compliant but are competitive advantages for the Group

You are a seasoned professional who understands that process is the heartbeat of a business. You have a proven track record of managing massive, multi-layered stakeholder environments and aren't afraid to challenge the status quo to ensure superior performance.

Sound good? Read on.

Here is a taster of what you can expect in this role:

Define the process vision, mission, objectives, goals, and associated KPIs and controls in alignment with the enterprise framework.
Hold direct accountability for the assigned end-to-end process, working with the relevant Business Owners, Data Owners, and Systems/Product Owners.
Lead and sponsor process improvement initiatives, including automation, simplification, and standardisation, to deliver efficiency and value.
Facilitate governance forums, monitor performance and compliance, and provide governance oversight for process changes driven by transformation initiatives.
Play a key role in rolling out and championing process governance and supporting frameworks across the groups and with your business partners 
Partner with business units and IT to drive alignment, accountability, and ownership of process activities, acting as the key escalation point for process-related issues, risks, or conflicts.
Oversee accurate and consistent process documentation within SAP Signavio (or similar platforms), ensuring adherence to enterprise standards.
Qualifications

Now let’s talk about you: 

You are a master of stakeholder diplomacy who can navigate complex matrixed environments, manage external service partners, and implement rigorous compliance controls that align with global regulatory standards.

A proven track record (typically 7-10+ years) of leading and transforming complex, global enterprise processes (e.g., Lead-to-Cash, Source-to-Pay, or Record-to-Report).
Bachelor’s degree in Business, IT, Engineering, or a related discipline.
Proven experience in business process management and transformation having completed similar roles as a process or global process owner 
The ability to navigate and influence a matrixed organization. You are comfortable acting as the single point of accountability and can manage expectations between internal business units and external service providers.
Certification in Lean Six Sigma or an equivalent process improvement methodology.
Proven expertise in SAP Signavio or similar process management platforms.
Demonstrated capability with industry-standard process modelling frameworks (BPMN 2.0, BPML, EPC, APQC PCF, etc.).
Experience working effectively within an outsourced service model environment.
Experience in Retail, FMCG, and Hospitality industries (desirable)
Experience in large-scale corporate environments, particularly with IT-enabled transformations (desirable)
Exposure to formal governance, compliance, and risk management frameworks (desirable)

Endeavour Group is full of opportunities - use our dedicated learning and development options to grow an idea, yourself, and your career. This is just the start, so dream big.

The Company

Our client is a market-leading ASX-listed property investment group with a high-profile leadership team, strong access to institutional capital and a clearly defined growth strategy. The business is highly acquisitive, continues to launch new funds and manages a large and diversified portfolio spanning premium Commercial, Industrial and Retail assets.

The Role

The Group FP&A Manager sits at the centre of financial planning, analysis and strategic decision-making for the organisation. Working closely with senior leadership and teams across the business, the role supports the delivery of group forecasting, budgeting, performance analysis and capital planning.

Key Responsibilities
Partner with divisional teams to understand key drivers of performance, risks and opportunities, and provide commercial insights.
Maintain and enhance group forecasting models across the income statement, balance sheet and cash flow.
Coordinate and deliver the group budgeting and long-term forecasting process, including consolidation of divisional inputs.
Support capital management through analysis of funding requirements, debt metrics and financial data for rating agencies.
Contribute to monthly and quarterly reporting for senior leadership, including analysis and commentary on group performance.
Assist with preparation of external reporting materials including results presentations, investor updates and other market communications.
Identify and implement improvements to financial planning tools, processes and reporting frameworks.
Skills & Experience
CA/CPA/CFA qualified.
5+ years’ experience in FP&A, financial planning, corporate finance or a similar analytical role.
Strong financial modelling, analytical and problem-solving skills.
Advanced Excel capability and strong presentation skills in PowerPoint and Word.
Experience working with large data sets and business intelligence tools such as Power BI is desirable.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills with the ability to translate financial insights for senior audiences.

About the company

A growing technology consultancy based in Sydney, focused on Data, Cloud, and AI solutions. This business supports clients with scalable, innovative technologies and offers a collaborative, fast-paced environment with space for growth and experimentation.

About the role

This Senior Data Engineer role sits within a small, high-performing team. You’ll take the lead on delivering Snowflake-centric data engineering solutions—from solution design and architecture through to implementation and optimisation. You’ll mentor junior engineers, guide best practices, and work across modern cloud platforms that heavily leverage Snowflake as the core data environment.

Benefits
$160,000 + Super
Work with cutting-edge Snowflake, cloud, and AI technologies
Supportive and innovative team culture
Flexible working arrangements
Opportunities to grow and take ownership of solutions
Duties/Key responsibilities
Design, build, and optimise robust Snowflake data pipelines, models, and warehouse solutions
Lead client delivery engagements and manage key stakeholder relationships
Mentor junior team members and champion Snowflake and cloud engineering best practices
Work across cloud-based platforms including AWS / GCP and Snowflake, with exposure to Databricks where relevant
Skills and experience
Strong experience in Snowflake architecture, data modelling, ETL/ELT processes, and scalable data pipelines
Proficient in Python, SQL, and/or Spark
Hands-on experience with Snowflake and complementary cloud environments such as Google Cloud or Databricks
Comfortable working directly with clients in consultative, solution-focused engagements
Knowledge of visualisation tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) and DevOps practices
Bonus: experience with Snowpipe, streaming data, big data frameworks, or data governance and security best practices
